jokes.text: I can't believe we're only 12 feet from the parking lot.
jokes.setup: Character expressing amazement at their war reenactment situation
jokes.punchline: The 'battlefield' is absurdly close to mundane parking lot
jokes.explanation: Good economy and structure, punctures the pretense of war reenactment with mundane reality

jokes.text: We're reenacting the Sith-Al War.
jokes.setup: Someone thinks they're doing Civil War reenactment
jokes.punchline: It's actually 'Sith-Al War' - a Star Wars pun on 'Civil War'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining Star Wars with Civil War, fits Futurama's sci-fi comedy tone

jokes.text: What the Hoth?
jokes.setup: Character wants to express confusion/surprise
jokes.punchline: Uses 'Hoth' (Star Wars planet) instead of 'Hell' in common expression
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Star Wars reference substitution, maintains the Sith-Al War theme

jokes.text: Darth Stroyer? That's the stupidest name I've ever heard.
jokes.setup: Character introduces intimidating Sith name 'Darth Stroyer'
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately dismisses it as stupid, deflating the drama
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Bender's blunt honesty, deflates pretentious Star Wars parody

jokes.text: Darth Trocious. Darth Sploder. Darth Urderer. Darth It-head.
jokes.setup: After 'Darth Stroyer' was mocked, character lists more Sith names
jokes.punchline: Each name gets progressively lazier, ending with 'Darth It-head'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating structure showing increasingly lazy naming, 'Darth It-head' is wonderfully stupid

jokes.text: Don't fire till you see the greens of their eyes.
jokes.setup: Classic war movie line 'Don't fire till you see the whites of their eyes'
jokes.punchline: Modified for aliens with green eyes instead of white
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective variation on famous military phrase for sci-fi context

jokes.text: It saves on bullets.
jokes.setup: Military command about waiting to see enemy eyes before firing
jokes.punchline: Practical, mundane reason (saving bullets) for dramatic military tactic
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and subversion - takes romantic military tradition and makes it about budget concerns

jokes.text: The candle that burns twice as bright burns half as long. Mm-hmm.
jokes.setup: Bender delivers profound philosophical quote about mortality
jokes.punchline: Professor's dismissive 'Mm-hmm' deflates the attempted wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Bender's pretension and Professor's indifference

jokes.text: You're dead. No, I'm just very old.
jokes.setup: Someone declares Professor dead in the reenactment
jokes.punchline: Professor corrects that he's not acting dead, just actually very old
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and perfect character moment - Professor's extreme age is mistaken for death

jokes.text: Tell my mother... to pick me up outside Quizno's.
jokes.setup: Dramatic death scene with final words to mother
jokes.punchline: Mundane request about sandwich shop pickup instead of profound message
jokes.explanation: Classic bathos structure - builds dramatic dying words then deflates with mundane modern concern

jokes.text: Let's pick up the pace, people. At this point in the actual war, the death toll was 98 million.
jokes.setup: War reenactment is proceeding slowly with few casualties
jokes.punchline: Historical comparison reveals absurdly high death toll expectation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating massive fictional death toll as production scheduling problem

jokes.text: They're putting me in my Sunday suit and shoving me in a wooden box. Now they're lowering me into the cold, cold ground. Hello, here come the worms.
jokes.setup: Character acting out elaborate death scene
jokes.punchline: Increasingly graphic and uncomfortable burial description ending with worms
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of uncomfortable death imagery, builds awkward over-acting

jokes.text: If you were really dead, you wouldn't be laughing so hard.
jokes.setup: Character doing elaborate death scene performance
jokes.punchline: Practical observation that corpses don't laugh during their description
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation of the over-acting, logical observation

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, death's a big deal to you flesh bags, but in case you didn't notice, I'm a robot.
jokes.setup: Discussion about death and mortality
jokes.punchline: Bender dismisses death concerns because he's a robot (ironic given episode plot)
jokes.explanation: Good character moment and dramatic irony since the episode is about Bender's mortality

jokes.text: So? What if something heavy fell on you, like a church?
jokes.setup: Challenging Bender's claim that robots can't die
jokes.punchline: Specifically suggests a church falling on him as example
jokes.explanation: Absurd but specific example, 'church' adds unexpected religious element to robot death scenario

jokes.text: My wireless backup unit saves a copy of me every day, so if my body gets killed, big whoop, I just download into another body. I'm immortal, baby.
jokes.setup: Bender explaining why he doesn't fear death
jokes.punchline: Confident claim of immortality (ironic given episode premise)
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's central irony - Bender's overconfidence about immortality

jokes.text: What?! Then how come you always scream so much when you're in danger?
jokes.setup: Bender claims he's immortal and doesn't fear death
jokes.punchline: Fry points out Bender's constant screaming contradicts this claim
jokes.explanation: Good character observation that calls out Bender's inconsistent behavior

jokes.text: I never said I wasn't a drama queen.
jokes.setup: Fry questioning why Bender screams if he's immortal
jokes.punchline: Bender admits to being a drama queen, explaining the inconsistency
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bender's self-awareness about his dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: Hurry up, you number-crunching crumbnuncher.
jokes.setup: Someone waiting impatiently for calculation results
jokes.punchline: 'Crumbnuncher' as creative insult for someone doing math
jokes.explanation: Creative compound insult with good alliteration, feels inventive

jokes.text: The winners, at a net cost of only $12 per life cut tragically short, our Sith overlords.
jokes.setup: Announcing war reenactment results
jokes.punchline: Treating fictional deaths as cost-benefit analysis with specific dollar amount
jokes.explanation: Dark humor reducing tragic fictional deaths to mundane accounting, specific price makes it funnier

jokes.text: Hot diggity. Oh, I mean... Supreme diggity.
jokes.setup: Character celebrating Sith victory
jokes.punchline: Self-corrects casual expression to be more 'Sith-appropriate'
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment of staying in theme, simple but effective wordplay

jokes.text: It may have been a fake war, but my scuffed knee is all too real.
jokes.setup: Reflecting on the war reenactment experience
jokes.punchline: Contrasts fake war with minor real injury in overly dramatic way
jokes.explanation: Good bathos structure and character moment for Fry's tendency to overdramatize minor problems

jokes.text: I've got pains in joints I had removed a century ago.
jokes.setup: Professor complaining about aches and pains
jokes.punchline: Claims to feel pain in joints he no longer has
jokes.explanation: Great character moment highlighting Professor's extreme age and medical history, absurd but believable for him

jokes.text: Your wheelchair? I don't need a wheelchair! The one with the wheels.
jokes.setup: Bender offers to get Professor's 'soft chair with wheels'
jokes.punchline: Professor insists it's not a wheelchair, just 'the one with wheels'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Professor's denial about his frailty, semantic evasion is funny

jokes.text: Inspector Five, the best inspector a kid could want.
jokes.setup: Bender about to show proof of his perfection
jokes.punchline: Describes quality control inspector with parental affection
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Bender's attachment to his inspector, humanizes a bureaucratic relationship

jokes.text: Hey, Mr. Perfect, you wet the chair.
jokes.setup: Bender just proclaimed his perfection with official documentation
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicted by evidence of malfunction (leaking oil)
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and structure - immediately deflates Bender's claims with physical evidence

jokes.text: Must be some of that urine you're all so proud of.
jokes.setup: Bender caught leaking oil
jokes.punchline: Tries to blame humans for the wetness with sarcastic comment about urine
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's denial and deflection tactics

jokes.text: You call that an ink defense?
jokes.setup: Bender spraying oil everywhere while malfunctioning
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'ink defense' (like octopus) while literally spraying fluid
jokes.explanation: Clever pun connecting octopus defense mechanism to robot oil leak, good economy

jokes.text: Eh, fatal, shmatal. If I die, I can just download my backup copy into a new, equally fabulous body.
jokes.setup: Professor says Bender has a fatal defect
jokes.punchline: Bender dismisses death casually, confident in backup plan
jokes.explanation: Callbacks to earlier immortality claim, 'equally fabulous body' is peak Bender narcissism

jokes.text: That's just it. You can't. You were built without a backup unit.
jokes.setup: Bender's confident dismissal of death due to backup capability
jokes.punchline: Professor reveals Bender has no backup - he can actually die
jokes.explanation: Major plot revelation that undercuts Bender's confidence, well-structured setup/payoff

jokes.setup: Learning he can actually die
jokes.punchline: Extended dramatic 'No!' reaction (likely with visual comedy)
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic reaction beat, funny through Bender's over-the-top response

jokes.text: Between a minute and a billion years.
jokes.setup: Asked how much time Bender has left to live
jokes.punchline: Uselessly broad timeframe that provides no helpful information
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - technically accurate but completely unhelpful, delivered deadpan

jokes.text: Well, at least you can plan accordingly.
jokes.setup: Professor's uselessly broad time estimate for Bender's death
jokes.punchline: Leela's sarcastic comment about impossible-to-use information
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback that highlights absurdity of Professor's answer

jokes.text: Violent outbursts. General sluttiness.
jokes.setup: Bender asks how living beings cope with mortality
jokes.punchline: Leela lists two unexpectedly blunt coping mechanisms
jokes.explanation: Unexpected frankness about mortality coping, good economy and surprising choices

jokes.text: I'll give you a nice Kevorking.
jokes.setup: Bender wanting suicide due to mortality fears
jokes.punchline: Professor offers 'Kevorking' - pun on Dr. Kevorkian (assisted suicide doctor)
jokes.explanation: Dark humor reference to assisted suicide, fits Professor's character

jokes.text: I beg to differ. Stinkin' bureaucrats... I hate 'em!
jokes.setup: Discussion about anonymous bureaucrats
jokes.punchline: Hermes (himself a bureaucrat) angrily denounces bureaucrats
jokes.explanation: Good ironic character moment - Hermes hating his own profession, shows self-awareness

jokes.text: Old pal? 8.5 seconds ago, you said you hated me.
jokes.setup: Bender suddenly calling Hermes 'old pal' after insulting bureaucrats
jokes.punchline: Hermes' precise timing of Bender's attitude change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes moment with precise timing measurement, calls out Bender's opportunism

jokes.text: Will you be taking your portable filing cabinet?
jokes.setup: Hermes claiming he's not just a bureaucrat
jokes.punchline: Leela immediately undercuts this with question about his filing cabinet
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Hermes' claims, highlights his obsessive bureaucratic habits

jokes.text: None of your beeswax.
jokes.setup: Leela asking about filing cabinet
jokes.punchline: Hermes' defensive response using childish phrase
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Hermes caught and defensive about his bureaucratic nature

jokes.text: Shall we review alphabetical order? ♪ A-B-C-D... ♪
jokes.setup: Hermes leaving Amy to do filing
jokes.punchline: Treats Amy like a child who doesn't know the alphabet
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hermes' bureaucratic obsessiveness and condescension

jokes.text: Uh... He's my pencil sharpener.
jokes.setup: Bureaucracy guard asking about Bender's purpose
jokes.punchline: Hermes claims Bender is a pencil sharpener to get him entry
jokes.explanation: Absurd but plausible lie in bureaucratic context, reducing Bender to office equipment

jokes.text: You betcha!
jokes.setup: Hermes claiming Bender is a pencil sharpener
jokes.punchline: Bender enthusiastically plays along with the deception
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's willingness to go along with schemes

jokes.text: Until now, I thought giant cubes were exciting.
jokes.setup: Viewing the massive Central Bureaucracy building
jokes.punchline: Bender's disappointed realization about cube architecture
jokes.explanation: Good deflation joke about bureaucratic architecture being boring even to robots

jokes.text: I did like the part where they screamed.
jokes.setup: Discussion about boring bureaucratic procedures
jokes.punchline: Bender's only enjoyment came from others' suffering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - finding joy in others' misery, economical delivery

jokes.text: Sorry, Hermes, I drank all your white-out.
jokes.setup: Hermes arriving at his weekend bureaucracy cubicle
jokes.punchline: Colleague casually mentions drinking correction fluid
jokes.explanation: Absurd casual reference to consuming office supplies, fits bureaucratic setting

jokes.text: Also, you have a rectangular mass in your colon.
jokes.setup: Bureaucracy scanner confirming Hermes' identity
jokes.punchline: Unnecessary medical information revealed by overly thorough scan
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment about overly invasive bureaucratic technology

jokes.text: Sweet file-not-found-of-Puegot Sound.
jokes.setup: Hermes discovering Inspector Five's record is missing
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic oath combining computer error with geographic location
jokes.explanation: Creative Hermes oath combining computer terminology with geography, fits his character

jokes.text: I recommend the men's room at the TWA terminal.
jokes.setup: Bender asking how to find anonymous guy he doesn't know
jokes.punchline: Hermes suggests airport bathroom (implying cruising)
jokes.explanation: Adult humor reference, somewhat out of character for Hermes but economical

jokes.text: ♪ A-B-C-D... ♪ Well, this requires a little extra thought.
jokes.setup: Amy trying to file something alphabetically
jokes.punchline: Simple alphabetical filing stumps Amy despite singing the alphabet
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier alphabet scene, shows Amy's incompetence despite knowing the song

jokes.text: I'm a natural.
jokes.setup: Amy struggling with basic filing
jokes.punchline: Self-congratulatory comment despite obvious incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Amy's overconfidence and lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Those bastards won't know what leafed through them.
jokes.setup: Hermes preparing to search through physical files
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'leafed' (pages) instead of 'lived' in tough-guy saying
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun combining tough-guy attitude with bureaucratic action, perfect for Hermes

jokes.text: Inspector One, Two, Three, Four, Six! There's no trace of Inspector Five!
jokes.setup: Searching for Inspector Five in the files
jokes.punchline: Perfect sequence of inspectors except Five is conspicuously missing
jokes.explanation: Good pattern-based mystery setup, clear numerical sequence with obvious gap

jokes.text: Maybe if I kicked the asses of Inspector Two, plus Inspector Three...
jokes.setup: Can't find Inspector Five to beat up
jokes.punchline: Bender considers mathematical solution (2+3=5) to his revenge problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logic joke - treating inspector numbers like math problem, perfect Bender reasoning

jokes.text: Addition never solved anything, Mon.
jokes.setup: Bender's mathematical approach to revenge
jokes.punchline: Hermes' philosophical rejection of mathematics as solution
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan wisdom from Hermes, ironic given he ate calculator for math power

jokes.text: It's not a suicide booth, you lard ass. It's a phone booth.
jokes.setup: Bender apparently entering what looks like suicide booth
jokes.punchline: Hermes reveals it's actually an old phone booth
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection about suicide booth vs phone booth, 'lard ass' adds character

jokes.text: Who did you call, Dial-a-Bomb?
jokes.setup: Explosion occurs after Bender makes phone call
jokes.punchline: Hermes suggests Bender called bomb delivery service
jokes.explanation: Good connection between phone call and explosion, economical pun on dial-a services

jokes.text: You dumb cocktail shaker!
jokes.setup: Bender's mistake in calling robot company
jokes.punchline: Hermes insults Bender by comparing him to bartending equipment
jokes.explanation: Creative robot-specific insult, economical and fits the moment

jokes.text: I can't run anymore. I'll have to skip.
jokes.setup: Hermes and Bender fleeing from killbots
jokes.punchline: Hermes switches to childlike skipping as alternative to running
jokes.explanation: Absurd but logical solution to exhaustion, visual comedy of bureaucrat skipping from danger

jokes.text: A pile of dead bears can only mean one thing. But what?
jokes.setup: Hermes noting they need to reach pile of dead bears
jokes.punchline: Claims it can only mean one thing but doesn't know what
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist setup-punchline structure, confident ignorance is funny

jokes.text: Not without limbo music.
jokes.setup: Bender can't bend low enough to limbo under laser
jokes.punchline: Claims he needs limbo music to bend properly
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bender's name relates to bending, needs party atmosphere for functionality

jokes.text: Someone said fire.
jokes.setup: Characters in danger discussing their situation
jokes.punchline: Killbot activates on hearing trigger word 'fire'
jokes.explanation: Running gag about killbots responding to specific words, callback to earlier 'shoot'

jokes.text: 12 straight hours of limbo. I haven't done that since my honeymoon.
jokes.setup: Hermes and Bender finished extended limbo session
jokes.punchline: Hermes references his honeymoon in context of physical positioning
jokes.explanation: Adult humor callback to honeymoon activities, fits character's married status

jokes.text: Dios mio! A rock has fallen here in the outskirts of Tijuana.
jokes.setup: Bender threw stone to demonstrate distance
jokes.punchline: Stone actually lands in Tijuana, confirming the literal distance
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to stone throw joke, confirms the absurd literal interpretation worked

jokes.text: Told you.
jokes.setup: Stone successfully reached Tijuana
jokes.punchline: Bender's smug satisfaction at being literally correct
jokes.explanation: Perfect economical payoff, Bender's smugness about literal correctness

jokes.text: Ouch-o!
jokes.setup: Hermes trying to speak Spanish at border
jokes.punchline: Creates Spanish-sounding word 'Ouch-o' when hurt
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective fake Spanish wordplay, fits Hermes' character attempt at language

jokes.text: I also would have accepted a bribe.
jokes.setup: Guard accepting proper documentation at border
jokes.punchline: Casually admits he would have preferred corruption
jokes.explanation: Good corruption humor, deadpan admission of preferring illegal shortcuts

jokes.text: My birthplace. It's closed! There's not even a shrine to me with a gift shop, selling piñatas of me!
jokes.setup: Bender discovering his factory birthplace is closed
jokes.punchline: Escalates from disappointment to expecting shrine and merchandise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender narcissism escalation - expects tourist attraction status at birthplace

jokes.text: Looks like a half-eaten cheese diaper.
jokes.setup: Searching through Mexican garbage
jokes.punchline: Disgusting description of unidentifiable garbage item
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with creative disgusting imagery, fairly standard approach

jokes.text: His ass-ias is gracias!
jokes.setup: Finding Inspector Five's address
jokes.punchline: Bender's mangled Spanish pun combining 'address' with 'gracias'
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender linguistic confusion, attempts Spanish but mangles it characteristically

jokes.text: I'm gonna squeeze you out of there like Tijuana toothpaste!
jokes.setup: Bender threatening to extract someone from hiding
jokes.punchline: Creates Mexico-specific version of common expression
jokes.explanation: Good location-specific variation on 'squeeze like toothpaste', fits Bender's threatening style

jokes.text: You know what? You're right. He was just a stupid bureaucrat. A stupid, paper-pushing bureaucrat. A stupid I-dotting, chair-squashing...
jokes.setup: Bender being comforted about Inspector Five
jokes.punchline: Escalating insults that become increasingly specific and petty
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of bureaucratic insults, shows Bender working through anger

jokes.text: No offense, Hermes. You're not like that. I mean, you're exactly like that, but you're okay.
jokes.setup: Bender insulting bureaucrats with Hermes present
jokes.punchline: Tries to exclude Hermes then admits he's exactly the same but acceptable
jokes.explanation: Excellent backhanded compliment structure, perfect Bender logic and character relationship

jokes.text: Someone said Howitzer!
jokes.setup: Killbots responding to trigger words throughout episode
jokes.punchline: Even more powerful weapon name triggers bigger response
jokes.explanation: Continues running gag about trigger words, escalates to bigger weapon

jokes.text: Bender terminated. Bender's saved. Cease fire.
jokes.setup: Kill-bot announcing mission completion
jokes.punchline: Contradiction between 'terminated' and 'saved' in same breath
jokes.explanation: Good robotic logic contradiction - termination equals salvation

jokes.text: And he's not looking back at that cool explosion. He's a hero!
jokes.setup: Hermes walking away from explosion he caused
jokes.punchline: Bender references action movie trope of not looking at explosions
jokes.explanation: Standard action movie trope reference, recognizable but not particularly original

jokes.text: and Zoidberg's getting overcooked!
jokes.setup: Amy listing problems at Planet Express
jokes.punchline: Includes Zoidberg being literally cooked as mundane office problem
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment treating Zoidberg's cooking as routine office problem

jokes.text: Too much papers! Not enough hiding plants!
jokes.setup: Office chaos due to Hermes' absence
jokes.punchline: Leela's stressed explanation mixing office problems
jokes.explanation: Shows office falling apart without Hermes, slightly broken English adds to stress

jokes.text: I've smoked a lot a paperwork in my day.
jokes.setup: Complimenting Hermes on his organizational skills
jokes.punchline: Hermes uses wrong verb 'smoked' instead of 'filed' or 'processed'
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment with language mix-up, fits Hermes' style

jokes.text: It's precious and sacred, and you only get so many millions of years on this Earth. So let's get brick-faced!
jokes.setup: Bender reflecting on life's precious and sacred nature
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggests getting extremely drunk as response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - philosophical wisdom immediately undercut by desire to drink, great contrast
